articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Growth Accelerator' Initiative to Propel Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'Green Growth Accelerator' (GGA) initiative, a multi-sectoral program aimed at integrating environmental sustainability with economic development.
• The initiative will focus on key areas including renewable energy expansion, green hydrogen production, sustainable agriculture, circular economy principles, and afforestation efforts.
• It involves significant public-private partnerships, incentivizing green technologies and investments in eco-friendly infrastructure.
• A dedicated Green Growth Fund will be established to finance projects and research, with an emphasis on job creation in green sectors.
• The GGA is expected to play a crucial role in India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and its net-zero targets.

Environmental impact assessment
Conservation
Economic development

articles.trivia: The concept of 'Green Growth' gained prominence after the 2008 financial crisis, advocating for environmentally sustainable economic progress.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Clarifies Data Fiduciary Responsibilities
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its provisions.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• Key clarifications were issued regarding the obligations of data fiduciaries, particularly concerning data localization, consent mechanisms, and cross-border data transfers.
• The ruling is expected to provide greater regulatory certainty for technology companies and enhance data privacy for Indian citizens.
• The judgment also highlighted the need for continuous technological adaptation in privacy frameworks.

Right to privacy
Cyber security

Judgment clarified responsibilities of data fiduciaries.
Emphasized balance between privacy rights and state interests.

Reinforces data privacy for citizens.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional implications of the Supreme Court's decision on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act.

articles.trivia: The 'right to privacy' was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

articles.headline: On Children's Day, NCPCR Launches 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces' Campaign
articles.explainer:
• Commemorating Children's Day, the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR) launched a nationwide campaign titled 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces'.
• The initiative aims to raise awareness among children, parents, and educators about online safety, cyberbullying prevention, and responsible digital citizenship.
• It includes workshops, educational modules, and a dedicated helpline for reporting online abuse and seeking guidance.
• The campaign emphasizes the role of technology in child development while mitigating associated risks.
• NCPCR reiterated its commitment to ensuring a safe and nurturing environment for children in both physical and virtual realms.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Maritime Security and Blue Economy Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded in Jakarta, with leaders adopting a joint declaration focused on enhancing cooperation in maritime security and the blue economy.
• The declaration emphasizes collaborative efforts to combat piracy, ensure freedom of navigation, and promote sustainable exploitation of marine resources in the Indo-Pacific region.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to ASEAN centrality and its Act East Policy, highlighting shared values and interests in regional peace and prosperity.
• Discussions also covered trade facilitation, digital connectivity, and climate change adaptation strategies.
• The leaders agreed to establish a new working group dedicated to exploring renewable energy potential in coastal areas.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures; Focus on Credit Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, citing easing retail inflation and stable growth prospects.
• This marks the fifth consecutive meeting where the MPC has maintained the status quo, indicating a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to support credit growth, particularly for MSMEs and infrastructure projects, to sustain economic momentum.
• Projections for Q3 FY26 indicate retail inflation to be within the comfort zone of 4-6%, while GDP growth is expected to remain robust.
• The RBI emphasized its commitment to remaining agile and ready to act if inflationary pressures re-emerge or growth falters.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of the RLV to a runway after atmospheric re-entry.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing space launch costs.
• The RLV program is critical for India's long-term space ambitions, including human spaceflight missions and commercial satellite launches.
• ISRO plans further advanced tests, including autonomous take-off and landing, in the coming years.

Examine the challenges involved in developing and operationalizing a fully reusable launch system.

articles.trivia: The first experimental flight of ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) was conducted in 2016, marking a significant step towards developing a winged reusable launch vehicle.

articles.headline: Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot Faces New Threats from Climate Change, Conservation Efforts Intensified
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) highlights increased vulnerability of the Western Ghats to climate change impacts, including altered rainfall patterns and rising temperatures.
• The report identifies specific regions within the biodiversity hotspot experiencing accelerated habitat fragmentation and species loss due to climate-induced stresses.
• In response, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced intensified conservation efforts, focusing on ecological restoration, climate-resilient species management, and community participation.
• Special emphasis will be placed on protecting endemic species and critical wildlife corridors.
• The initiative calls for greater interstate cooperation among the six states that share the Western Ghats region.

articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes New National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Framework for Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Education today released the finalized implementation framework for the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, specifically focusing on higher education reforms.
• The framework outlines detailed guidelines for multidisciplinary institutions, credit transfer mechanisms, academic bank of credits, and flexible learning pathways.
• It emphasizes faculty development, research promotion, and internationalization of higher education institutions.
• States and universities are urged to expedite the adoption of these guidelines to achieve the NEP's vision of holistic and accessible higher education.
• Key initiatives include establishing more research universities and promoting vocational education integration at the tertiary level.

articles.headline: Dedicated Freight Corridor Project Achieves Major Milestone, Boosting Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) project in India today announced the operationalization of over 90% of its Western and Eastern DFC lines.
• This major milestone significantly enhances the country's logistics efficiency, reducing transit times for freight movement and decongesting existing railway networks.
• The DFCs are equipped with state-of-the-art signaling and communication systems, allowing for higher speeds and increased carrying capacity.
• The project is crucial for boosting industrial growth, facilitating trade, and reducing the carbon footprint of transportation.
• The remaining sections are expected to be fully operational within the next year, further integrating India's industrial hubs with ports.

articles.headline: India Participates in Multi-National Cyber Security Exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025'
articles.explainer:
• India's cyber security agencies, including CERT-In, participated in the multi-national exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025', aimed at enhancing collective resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
• The exercise simulated real-world cyberattacks on critical infrastructure, focusing on information sharing, coordinated response strategies, and incident management protocols.
• Participation in such exercises underscores India's growing role in global cyber security cooperation and its commitment to a free, open, and secure cyberspace.
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology highlighted the importance of international collaboration in combating cross-border cybercrime and state-sponsored attacks.
• Key takeaways included identifying vulnerabilities in emerging technologies and refining national cyber defense doctrines.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) today announced a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, particularly targeting underserved rural and remote areas across India.
• This expansion aims to bridge the healthcare access gap by leveraging digital platforms for consultations, diagnostics, and prescription delivery.
• New partnerships with community health centers (CHCs) and primary health centers (PHCs) are being forged to integrate telemedicine into the existing rural healthcare infrastructure.
• The initiative focuses on enhancing digital literacy among rural healthcare workers and patients to maximize the utilization of these services.
• It is expected to improve health outcomes, reduce travel burden, and provide specialized medical advice in areas lacking adequate medical professionals.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Circular Economy in E-Waste Management Announced
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework promoting a circular economy approach for e-waste management in India.
• The policy emphasizes extended producer responsibility (EPR), incentivizing manufacturers to design products for durability, repairability, and recyclability.
• It aims to formalize the informal e-waste recycling sector, ensuring safer working conditions and environmentally sound disposal practices.
• The framework includes provisions for digital tracking of e-waste, consumer awareness campaigns, and research & development in recycling technologies.
• This move is crucial for reducing environmental pollution, conserving valuable resources, and creating green jobs.

articles.headline: Panchayati Raj Institutions Empowered with Enhanced Digital Tools for Grassroots Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj today launched an upgraded suite of digital tools and platforms aimed at empowering Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) for more efficient and transparent grassroots governance.
• The new tools integrate features for online grievance redressal, real-time tracking of development projects, digital financial management, and citizen participation platforms.
• This initiative builds upon the e-Panchayat Mission Mode Project, further enhancing digital literacy and capacity building among elected representatives and local officials.
• The move is expected to improve service delivery, foster greater accountability, and strengthen local self-governance in rural India.
• Training programs and technical support will be provided to ensure widespread adoption and effective utilization of the new digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Drought-Resistant Crop Varieties Developed by Indian Agricultural Scientists
articles.explainer:
• Indian agricultural scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have announced a significant breakthrough in developing new genetically modified drought-resistant crop varieties.
• These new varieties, primarily focusing on staple crops like rice, wheat, and maize, exhibit enhanced water-use efficiency and resilience to prolonged dry spells.
• The development is crucial for ensuring food security in the face of increasing climate variability and water scarcity across various regions of India.
• Field trials have shown promising results in terms of yield stability under drought conditions, offering a potential solution for farmers in arid and semi-arid zones.
• The government plans to fast-track the regulatory approval process for commercial cultivation, while also ensuring proper biosafety assessments.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of extensive remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has unearthed stupas, prayer halls, monastic cells, and numerous artifacts including Buddhist sculptures and terracotta seals dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread and flourishing of Buddhism in ancient Kalinga (modern-day Odisha) and its cultural links with other Buddhist centers.
• The site is believed to be part of a larger network of Buddhist establishments, similar to the well-known Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri complexes.
• Conservation efforts are underway, and plans are being made to develop the site for archaeological study and cultural tourism.

The history of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.- their forms and effect on the society.

articles.trivia: Odisha is home to the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites: Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri, known for their rich monastic complexes.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Focus on Services Sector
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and critical phase, with both sides expressing optimism for a swift conclusion.
• The current round of talks is primarily focused on achieving breakthroughs in market access for services, including financial services, legal services, and digital services.
• India is keen on securing greater mobility for its skilled professionals, while the UK seeks enhanced access for its products and investments.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and consumers in both countries.
• Despite some sticking points on tariffs for certain goods and intellectual property rights, negotiators are confident of finding common ground.

articles.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India actively championed the adoption and interoperability of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the success of its own DPIs like UPI, Aadhaar, and ONDC in fostering financial inclusion and economic growth.
• Discussions focused on creating a global framework for DPIs to facilitate cross-border payments, enhance digital identity solutions, and bridge the digital divide in developing nations.
• Member countries acknowledged India's leadership in this domain and explored mechanisms for knowledge sharing and technical assistance.
• The move aims to build a more inclusive and resilient global financial architecture, leveraging technology for sustainable development.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Indian Antarctic Bill, 2025, Strengthening Research and Environmental Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Antarctic Bill, 2025, was passed by both houses of Parliament today, providing a comprehensive legal framework for India's activities in the Antarctic region.
• The Bill aims to regulate visits and activities to Antarctica, protect the Antarctic environment, and implement provisions of the Antarctic Treaty System.
• It establishes a robust regulatory mechanism for Indian expeditions, including permits for research, tourism, and resource exploration (prohibited by the Treaty).
• The legislation also extends the jurisdiction of Indian courts to crimes committed by Indian citizens in Antarctica, ensuring accountability.
• This landmark Bill underscores India's commitment to responsible scientific research and environmental stewardship in the polar regions.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' for Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today launched the 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' (UFRM), a new scheme aimed at enhancing flood preparedness and mitigation in major Indian cities.
• The mission will focus on developing integrated urban flood management plans, upgrading drainage infrastructure, implementing early warning systems, and promoting sustainable urban planning.
• It involves collaboration between central, state, and local urban bodies, leveraging technology and community participation for effective implementation.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in ten high-risk cities, with plans for nationwide rollout based on their success.
• The UFRM seeks to minimize economic losses and human casualties caused by increasingly frequent and intense urban flooding events.

articles.headline: India Updates Critical Minerals Policy to Boost Domestic Exploration and Processing
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines today announced an updated National Critical Minerals Policy, aimed at boosting domestic exploration, mining, and processing of critical minerals.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation, simplified regulatory procedures, and strategic partnerships with mineral-rich nations.
• Critical minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements are vital for India's energy transition, digital economy, and defense sectors.
• The government plans to establish a Critical Minerals Research and Development Council to foster indigenous technologies for extraction and refining.
• This move is designed to reduce India's import dependence, enhance supply chain resilience, and position the country as a key player in the global critical minerals market.

articles.headline: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi to Include More Marginal Farmers and Landless Labourers
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, extending its benefits to more marginal farmers and, for the first time, to eligible landless agricultural labourers.
• The revamped scheme aims to provide greater financial support to vulnerable sections of the farming community, aligning with the government's commitment to doubling farmers' income.
• Eligibility criteria have been modified to include certain categories of landless labourers who depend on agriculture for their livelihood, using a robust verification mechanism.
• The move is expected to significantly enhance rural income and reduce agrarian distress across the country.
• An additional budgetary allocation has been approved to cover the expanded beneficiary base.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Green Growth Accelerator' Initiative:
1. It is a multi-sectoral program focusing exclusively on renewable energy expansion.
2. A dedicated Green Growth Fund will be established to finance projects.
3. The initiative aims to integrate environmental sustainability with economic development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as the initiative is multi-sectoral, focusing on renewable energy, green hydrogen, sustainable agriculture, circular economy, and afforestation. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as a dedicated Green Growth Fund will be established, and the initiative aims to integrate environmental sustainability with economic development.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, recently upheld by the Supreme Court, primarily deals with:
(a) Regulation of digital advertising and e-commerce platforms.
(b) Safeguarding the privacy of individuals' personal data in the digital realm.
(c) Preventing cyber warfare and state-sponsored cyberattacks.
(d) Ensuring fair use of copyrighted digital content.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Safeguarding the privacy of individuals' personal data in the digital realm.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is a landmark legislation in India aimed at providing a comprehensive legal framework for the protection of digital personal data. Its core objective is to safeguard the privacy of individuals' personal data and regulate how entities collect, process, and store such data.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following bodies launched the 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces' Campaign on Children's Day 2025?
(a) Ministry of Women and Child Development
(b) Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
(c) National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR)
(d) National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences (NIMHANS)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR)
prelimsMocks.explanation: On Children's Day, the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR) launched the nationwide campaign 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces' to promote online safety and digital citizenship for children.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program:
1. The program aims to drastically reduce space launch costs.
2. The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating atmospheric re-entry.
3. It is being developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The RLV program aims to reduce launch costs, and the recent test involved a controlled landing. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Reusable Launch Vehicle program is being developed by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), not DRDO.

prelimsMocks.question:
The term 'Blue Economy', as discussed in the India-ASEAN Summit, primarily refers to:
(a) Economic activities related to the sustainable use of ocean resources.
(b) The financial sector's contribution to environmental conservation.
(c) The development of coastal tourism infrastructure.
(d) International trade agreements focused on marine products.
prelimsMocks.answer: (a) Economic activities related to the sustainable use of ocean resources.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Blue Economy encompasses a range of economic activities that are related to oceans, seas, and coasts, focusing on their sustainable use for economic growth, improved livelihoods, and ocean ecosystem health. In the India-ASEAN context, it involves cooperation in sustainable exploitation of marine resources.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the 'Green Growth Accelerator' initiative. How far can such comprehensive policies help India achieve its dual goals of rapid economic development and ambitious climate targets? (150 words)

Analyze its potential for economic growth (job creation, green tech investment) and climate goals (NDCs, net-zero). Conclude with challenges (funding, implementation, coordination) and the necessity of such integrated approaches.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step towards data privacy in India. Discuss the key features of the Act and evaluate its effectiveness in balancing individual privacy rights with national security and legitimate state interests. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies; Right to privacy, Cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the significance of the SC judgment. Outline key features of DPDP Act: consent, data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, cross-border data transfer rules, penalties. Discuss the balancing act: how it protects privacy (consent, data minimization) while allowing legitimate state access (national security, public order). Evaluate effectiveness by considering implementation challenges, technological advancements, and comparisons with global standards (e.g., GDPR). Conclude on the evolving nature of privacy laws in a digital age.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent efforts in developing indigenous space technology, as exemplified by the Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program, are crucial for its strategic autonomy. Discuss the strategic and economic implications of such advancements for India. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.

Discuss economic implications: significant reduction in launch costs, commercial opportunities in satellite launches, growth of the domestic space industry, job creation, and technology spin-offs. Conclude by highlighting India's growing stature in global space economy and technology.

GS1.headline: On Children's Day, NCPCR Launches 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces' Campaign
GS1.explainer:
• Commemorating Children's Day, the National Commission for Protection of Child Rights (NCPCR) launched a nationwide campaign titled 'Child-Friendly Digital Spaces'.
• The initiative aims to raise awareness among children, parents, and educators about online safety, cyberbullying prevention, and responsible digital citizenship.
• It includes workshops, educational modules, and a dedicated helpline for reporting online abuse and seeking guidance.
• The campaign emphasizes the role of technology in child development while mitigating associated risks.
• NCPCR reiterated its commitment to ensuring a safe and nurturing environment for children in both physical and virtual realms.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of extensive remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has unearthed stupas, prayer halls, monastic cells, and numerous artifacts including Buddhist sculptures and terracotta seals dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread and flourishing of Buddhism in ancient Kalinga (modern-day Odisha) and its cultural links with other Buddhist centers.
• The site is believed to be part of a larger network of Buddhist establishments, similar to the well-known Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri complexes.
• Conservation efforts are underway, and plans are being made to develop the site for archaeological study and cultural tourism.

GS1.trivia: Odisha is home to the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites: Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri, known for their rich monastic complexes.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Clarifies Data Fiduciary Responsibilities
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its provisions.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• Key clarifications were issued regarding the obligations of data fiduciaries, particularly concerning data localization, consent mechanisms, and cross-border data transfers.
• The ruling is expected to provide greater regulatory certainty for technology companies and enhance data privacy for Indian citizens.
• The judgment also highlighted the need for continuous technological adaptation in privacy frameworks.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Joint Declaration on Maritime Security and Blue Economy Cooperation
GS2.explainer:

• The declaration emphasizes collaborative efforts to combat piracy, ensure freedom of navigation, and promote sustainable exploitation of marine resources in the Indo-Pacific region.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to ASEAN centrality and its Act East Policy, highlighting shared values and interests in regional peace and prosperity.
• Discussions also covered trade facilitation, digital connectivity, and climate change adaptation strategies.
• The leaders agreed to establish a new working group dedicated to exploring renewable energy potential in coastal areas.

GS2.headline: Government Finalizes New National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Framework for Higher Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Education today released the finalized implementation framework for the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, specifically focusing on higher education reforms.
• The framework outlines detailed guidelines for multidisciplinary institutions, credit transfer mechanisms, academic bank of credits, and flexible learning pathways.
• It emphasizes faculty development, research promotion, and internationalization of higher education institutions.
• States and universities are urged to expedite the adoption of these guidelines to achieve the NEP's vision of holistic and accessible higher education.
• Key initiatives include establishing more research universities and promoting vocational education integration at the tertiary level.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:

• This expansion aims to bridge the healthcare access gap by leveraging digital platforms for consultations, diagnostics, and prescription delivery.
• New partnerships with community health centers (CHCs) and primary health centers (PHCs) are being forged to integrate telemedicine into the existing rural healthcare infrastructure.
• The initiative focuses on enhancing digital literacy among rural healthcare workers and patients to maximize the utilization of these services.
• It is expected to improve health outcomes, reduce travel burden, and provide specialized medical advice in areas lacking adequate medical professionals.

GS2.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Focus on Services Sector
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and critical phase, with both sides expressing optimism for a swift conclusion.
• The current round of talks is primarily focused on achieving breakthroughs in market access for services, including financial services, legal services, and digital services.
• India is keen on securing greater mobility for its skilled professionals, while the UK seeks enhanced access for its products and investments.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and consumers in both countries.
• Despite some sticking points on tariffs for certain goods and intellectual property rights, negotiators are confident of finding common ground.

• Member countries acknowledged India's leadership in this domain and explored mechanisms for knowledge sharing and technical assistance.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Indian Antarctic Bill, 2025, Strengthening Research and Environmental Protection
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian Antarctic Bill, 2025, was passed by both houses of Parliament today, providing a comprehensive legal framework for India's activities in the Antarctic region.
• The Bill aims to regulate visits and activities to Antarctica, protect the Antarctic environment, and implement provisions of the Antarctic Treaty System.
• It establishes a robust regulatory mechanism for Indian expeditions, including permits for research, tourism, and resource exploration (prohibited by the Treaty).
• The legislation also extends the jurisdiction of Indian courts to crimes committed by Indian citizens in Antarctica, ensuring accountability.
• This landmark Bill underscores India's commitment to responsible scientific research and environmental stewardship in the polar regions.

GS2.trivia: India has two operational research stations in Antarctica: Maitri and Bharati, with the first station, Dakshin Gangotri, now serving as a supply base.

GS2.headline: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi to Include More Marginal Farmers and Landless Labourers
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved an expansion of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, extending its benefits to more marginal farmers and, for the first time, to eligible landless agricultural labourers.
• The revamped scheme aims to provide greater financial support to vulnerable sections of the farming community, aligning with the government's commitment to doubling farmers' income.
• Eligibility criteria have been modified to include certain categories of landless labourers who depend on agriculture for their livelihood, using a robust verification mechanism.
• The move is expected to significantly enhance rural income and reduce agrarian distress across the country.
• An additional budgetary allocation has been approved to cover the expanded beneficiary base.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Green Growth Accelerator' Initiative to Propel Sustainable Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'Green Growth Accelerator' (GGA) initiative, a multi-sectoral program aimed at integrating environmental sustainability with economic development.
• The initiative will focus on key areas including renewable energy expansion, green hydrogen production, sustainable agriculture, circular economy principles, and afforestation efforts.
• It involves significant public-private partnerships, incentivizing green technologies and investments in eco-friendly infrastructure.
• A dedicated Green Growth Fund will be established to finance projects and research, with an emphasis on job creation in green sectors.
• The GGA is expected to play a crucial role in India's commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement and its net-zero targets.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures; Focus on Credit Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, citing easing retail inflation and stable growth prospects.
• This marks the fifth consecutive meeting where the MPC has maintained the status quo, indicating a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to support credit growth, particularly for MSMEs and infrastructure projects, to sustain economic momentum.
• Projections for Q3 FY26 indicate retail inflation to be within the comfort zone of 4-6%, while GDP growth is expected to remain robust.
• The RBI emphasized its commitment to remaining agile and ready to act if inflationary pressures re-emerge or growth falters.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of the RLV to a runway after atmospheric re-entry.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing space launch costs.
• The RLV program is critical for India's long-term space ambitions, including human spaceflight missions and commercial satellite launches.
• ISRO plans further advanced tests, including autonomous take-off and landing, in the coming years.

GS3.headline: Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot Faces New Threats from Climate Change, Conservation Efforts Intensified
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) highlights increased vulnerability of the Western Ghats to climate change impacts, including altered rainfall patterns and rising temperatures.
• The report identifies specific regions within the biodiversity hotspot experiencing accelerated habitat fragmentation and species loss due to climate-induced stresses.
• In response, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced intensified conservation efforts, focusing on ecological restoration, climate-resilient species management, and community participation.
• Special emphasis will be placed on protecting endemic species and critical wildlife corridors.
• The initiative calls for greater interstate cooperation among the six states that share the Western Ghats region.

GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: Dedicated Freight Corridor Project Achieves Major Milestone, Boosting Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) project in India today announced the operationalization of over 90% of its Western and Eastern DFC lines.
• This major milestone significantly enhances the country's logistics efficiency, reducing transit times for freight movement and decongesting existing railway networks.
• The DFCs are equipped with state-of-the-art signaling and communication systems, allowing for higher speeds and increased carrying capacity.
• The project is crucial for boosting industrial growth, facilitating trade, and reducing the carbon footprint of transportation.
• The remaining sections are expected to be fully operational within the next year, further integrating India's industrial hubs with ports.

GS3.headline: India Participates in Multi-National Cyber Security Exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025'
GS3.explainer:
• India's cyber security agencies, including CERT-In, participated in the multi-national exercise 'Cyber Shield 2025', aimed at enhancing collective resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
• The exercise simulated real-world cyberattacks on critical infrastructure, focusing on information sharing, coordinated response strategies, and incident management protocols.
• Participation in such exercises underscores India's growing role in global cyber security cooperation and its commitment to a free, open, and secure cyberspace.
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology highlighted the importance of international collaboration in combating cross-border cybercrime and state-sponsored attacks.
• Key takeaways included identifying vulnerabilities in emerging technologies and refining national cyber defense doctrines.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Circular Economy in E-Waste Management Announced
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework promoting a circular economy approach for e-waste management in India.
• The policy emphasizes extended producer responsibility (EPR), incentivizing manufacturers to design products for durability, repairability, and recyclability.
• It aims to formalize the informal e-waste recycling sector, ensuring safer working conditions and environmentally sound disposal practices.
• The framework includes provisions for digital tracking of e-waste, consumer awareness campaigns, and research & development in recycling technologies.
• This move is crucial for reducing environmental pollution, conserving valuable resources, and creating green jobs.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Drought-Resistant Crop Varieties Developed by Indian Agricultural Scientists
GS3.explainer:
• Indian agricultural scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have announced a significant breakthrough in developing new genetically modified drought-resistant crop varieties.
• These new varieties, primarily focusing on staple crops like rice, wheat, and maize, exhibit enhanced water-use efficiency and resilience to prolonged dry spells.
• The development is crucial for ensuring food security in the face of increasing climate variability and water scarcity across various regions of India.
• Field trials have shown promising results in terms of yield stability under drought conditions, offering a potential solution for farmers in arid and semi-arid zones.
• The government plans to fast-track the regulatory approval process for commercial cultivation, while also ensuring proper biosafety assessments.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' for Major Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today launched the 'Urban Flood Resilience Mission' (UFRM), a new scheme aimed at enhancing flood preparedness and mitigation in major Indian cities.
• The mission will focus on developing integrated urban flood management plans, upgrading drainage infrastructure, implementing early warning systems, and promoting sustainable urban planning.
• It involves collaboration between central, state, and local urban bodies, leveraging technology and community participation for effective implementation.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in ten high-risk cities, with plans for nationwide rollout based on their success.
• The UFRM seeks to minimize economic losses and human casualties caused by increasingly frequent and intense urban flooding events.

GS3.headline: India Updates Critical Minerals Policy to Boost Domestic Exploration and Processing
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines today announced an updated National Critical Minerals Policy, aimed at boosting domestic exploration, mining, and processing of critical minerals.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation, simplified regulatory procedures, and strategic partnerships with mineral-rich nations.
• Critical minerals like Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements are vital for India's energy transition, digital economy, and defense sectors.
• The government plans to establish a Critical Minerals Research and Development Council to foster indigenous technologies for extraction and refining.
• This move is designed to reduce India's import dependence, enhance supply chain resilience, and position the country as a key player in the global critical minerals market.
